_comment: Importable Orcabot dashboard template (DashboardTemplateWithData). Inserted into D1 via benchmarks/slopcodebench/bin/insert-template or the templates API. The orchestrator's bootCommand appends a briefing to CLAUDE.md so Claude Code boots knowing the validated slop-code commands. Manual one-time VM setup (fork + venv) still required.
name: SlopCodeBench Runner
description: Run slop-code-bench arms with a Claude Code orchestrator you drive in English. One-time VM setup required (fork + venv).

items.content: {"name": "Benchmark Orchestrator", "agentic": true, "bootCommand": "B=''; grep -q SCB-ORCH CLAUDE.md 2>/dev/null || echo \"$B\" | base64 -d >> CLAUDE.md; exec claude", "workingDir": "slop-code-bench", "skipApprovals": true}

{"text": "# SlopCodeBench Runner

Run slop-code-bench arms with a **Claude Code orchestrator** you drive in plain English.

## One-time setup (per VM)
1. Fork at `/workspace/slop-code-bench` with venv `/workspace/scb-venv` (uv sync `--python 3.12`). On desktop this is the host-shared workspace.
2. Add your provider key in the **secrets panel**, broker-protected (e.g. `OPENROUTER_API_KEY`).

## Drive the orchestrator (it already knows these from CLAUDE.md)
- "Run `file_backup` with opencode on kimi-k2.6"
- "Show me the run log" / "is it past checkpoint_1 with cost>0?"
- "Score the finished run"

Validated arm: opencode + Kimi K2.6 via OpenRouter. Broker URL must be `127.0.0.1`, not `localhost`. Full docs: `benchmarks/slopcodebench/RESUME-kimi-opencode.md`."}

setupGuide:
# SlopCodeBench setup walkthrough

Goal: get this dashboard ready to run a slop-code-bench arm, then run one.
Be brief; do each step with your tools and confirm before the next.

1. **Choose the harness source.** Ask the user which to use:
   - (a) **Our fork** `https://github.com/robdmac/slop-code-bench` (branch
     `feat/host-tmux-executor`) — recommended; it has the no-Docker watchable
     executor + the broker-compatible routing this needs. Default to this.
   - (b) a **custom fork/branch URL** they paste.
   - (c) **already present** at `/workspace/slop-code-bench` — skip the clone.

2. **Clone it** (unless (c)) via terminal_input in a shell terminal:
   `git clone --branch <branch> <url> /workspace/slop-code-bench`
   (needs network; if a domain is held by the egress proxy, tell the user to approve it.)

3. **Create the venv** (one-time, slow): in `/workspace/slop-code-bench` run
   `UV_PYTHON_INSTALL_DIR=/workspace/.uv-python UV_PROJECT_ENVIRONMENT=/workspace/scb-venv uv sync --python 3.12`
   (Python 3.12 — 3.14 has no wheels and the VM has no compiler.)

4. **Provider key.** Ensure an `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` broker secret exists for this
   dashboard. If not, ask the user to add it (secrets_create, broker-protected).
   Never display or echo the value.

5. **Point the broker URL at 127.0.0.1** (not localhost — Node/opencode can't
   reach the IPv4-only broker via `localhost`). In the checkout:
   `cp configs/providers.yaml.orig configs/providers.yaml` then replace the
   openrouter `api_base` with `$OPENROUTER_BASE_URL` after swapping
   `localhost`->`127.0.0.1`.

6. **Run one arm** and watch it: launch detached to a logfile, then tail it:
   `/workspace/scb-venv/bin/slop-code run --agent opencode --model openrouter/kimi-k2.6 --environment configs/environments/local-tmux-py.yaml --prompt configs/prompts/just-solve.jinja --problem file_backup --no-evaluate thinking=low`
   Success = advances past `checkpoint_1` with `cost>0` and `steps>0`. Report when confirmed.
